name: Sequencer CHOP
description: '''NOTE''': '''The [[Timer CHOP]] replaces the Sequencer CHOP'''. Sequencer CHOP has been removed (it will still work in existing files). See the [[Timer CHOP]] examples in the OP Snippets (Help -> Operator Snippets). The Timer CHOP is an engine for running timed processes. It outputs channels such as timing fractions, counters, pulses and timer states, and it calls python functions (callbacks) on various timing events.

parameters.name: DAT List
parameters.label: DAT List
parameters.description: The table of CHOPs to sequence, the table contains the paths to the CHOPs to be sequenced.

parameters.name: Blend Scope
parameters.label: Blend Scope
parameters.description: Specifies which channels should blend between transitions, otherwise they add or jump.

parameters.name: Add Scope
parameters.label: Add Scope
parameters.description: Specifies which channels should add during transitions, otherwise they blend or jump.

parameters.name: Queue
parameters.label: Queue
parameters.description: Specifies the channel that controls when to queue (pause).

parameters.name: Trigger
parameters.label: Trigger
parameters.description: Begin transitioning immediately, instead of waiting until the end of the current CHOP channels.

parameters.name: Reset
parameters.label: Reset
parameters.description: While On, resets the sequence to the default CHOP.

_provenance.fact: scraped from the live Sequencer_CHOP wiki page
